differential_diagnoses: Viral exanthem (e.g., EBV or CMV infection)
next_steps: Perform a skin scraping for microscopic identification of Sarcoptes scabiei mites, eggs, or feces
Order serologic testing for syphilis (RPR and treponemal-specific test)
Obtain skin biopsy for histopathology and direct immunofluorescence if diagnosis remains elusive
Isolate the patient to prevent potential transmission until definitive diagnosis
Initiate empiric scabicidal therapy (e.g., permethrin 5% cream)
